sq SqueezyAgent
How it works Docs Benchmarks
GitHub
architecture draft

Spend local CPU before paid model context.

SqueezyAgent puts a deterministic local analysis layer between the user request and the LLM. The agent asks your codebase what connects to what, then sends the model targeted evidence instead of billable search noise.

01 Parse

Tree-sitter extracts declarations, imports, outlines, tests, and call-like syntax.

02 Index

A local index stores files, functions, modules, references, and tests.

03 Look up

The agent asks for ownership, impact, callers, tests, and likely code slices.

04 Pack

Results become small evidence packets with paths, hashes, and reasons.

05 Act

The model reads exact slices, plans a patch, applies it, and verifies.

local analysis

Useful structure, with clear limits.

SqueezyAgent does not need a full compiler or language server before it can save tokens. It can use fast static analysis to find likely files, functions, callers, and tests, then fall back to bounded text search when local structure is not enough.

symbol_context(AuthLayer)

decl crates/server/src/auth/layer.rs:18..44
callers router.rs:62, session.rs:101, tests/auth_flow.rs:29
tests 3 paired by module path and proximity
fallback uncertain edge; bounded text check suggested
what gets cheaper

Less paid search. More local evidence.

Repeated reads

Receipts let unchanged file ranges come back as compact references instead of full text.

Broad search

Local structure narrows the target before grep or raw reads enter the loop.

Tool chatter

Intent-level tools answer "who owns this" and "what breaks" without shell archaeology.

sq SqueezyAgent

A local static engine helps the agent understand code structure before wasting LLM tokens on search.
